rulings.Case Title: DARRELL RICHARDSON VS. BW/IP INTERNATIONAL INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 12, 2017, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Viad Corp.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's Negligence I and Products Liability causes of action are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication of plaintiff's products liability cause of action, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant did not attempt to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence, so defendant may only sustain its initial burden if it conclusively negated an element of plaintiff's cause of action. Defendant failed to do so. Defendant failed to present evidence demonstrating that it is not the successor-in-interest to Griscom-Russell Co. As to summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence I cause of action, defendant failed to demonstrate that the product line successor rule is the only potential exception that applies, so the discussion in Franklin v. USX Corp. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 615 regarding the inapplicability of such exception to ordinary negligence claims is not determinative. Plaintiff's objections to the testimony of Peter Novak are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWHEEN BRYANT VS. RILEY POWER INC ET AL

If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN HAHN ET AL VS. JOHN CRANE INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 12, 2017, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' complaint is denied. The complaint states sufficient facts to support a claim for punitive damages. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
